optimizations: Reduce wall thickness in areas where possible ($1.50 savings)
Simplify part geometry by eliminating unnecessary features or consolidating pockets ($2.00 savings)
Consider using a more efficient machining strategy (e.g., 4-axis) if the part's complexity warrants it ($3.00 savings)
risks: Deep pockets may require specialized tooling or longer machining time.
Thin walls (8 mm) could lead to vibration issues during machining, affecting surface finish and potentially requiring additional setup.
